senses.definition: a liquid used by cars, trucks, etc. as a fuel

examples.x: I need to stop at the gas station to fill up my car with gasoline.
He filled the tank with gasoline before heading on the road trip.
Prices for gasoline have gone up this month.
The car wouldn’t start because it ran out of gasoline.

senses.definition: a type of gasoline that does not contain lead additives, used in most modern vehicles

examples.x: Unleaded gasoline is required for vehicles equipped with catalytic converters.
She filled up her car with unleaded gasoline at the corner gas station.
The price of unleaded gasoline fluctuated based on market trends.
Unleaded gasoline is available in different octane ratings for performance.
They switched to unleaded gasoline to comply with environmental regulations.

senses.definition: a type of alcohol fuel produced from renewable sources such as corn or sugarcane

examples.x: Ethanol is blended with gasoline to create biofuels.
The ethanol content in gasoline was labeled as E85.
They researched the ethanol fuel economy compared to gasoline.
Ethanol fuel stations are designated by green pumps.
She supported ethanol production for its environmental benefits.

senses.definition: a type of fuel used in diesel engines, known for its high energy density and efficiency

examples.x: Diesel oil is used in heavy-duty trucks and construction equipment.
The diesel oil price fluctuated due to global demand.
He checked the diesel oil levels before starting the engine.
Diesel oil combustion produces more torque than gasoline engines.
The truck runs efficiently on diesel oil.

senses.definition: the natural gas that has been compressed to a high pressure for use as a fuel in vehicles

examples.x: Many public buses in urban areas now run on compressed natural gas due to its lower emissions compared to diesel.
Some taxi companies are switching their fleets to compressed natural gas to save on fuel costs.
Manufacturers are developing more efficient engines to run on compressed natural gas for future vehicle models.

senses.definition: a mixture of propane and butane gases stored under pressure as a liquid

examples.x: Liquefied petroleum gas is commonly used in household heating and cooking.

senses.definition: a clean and renewable fuel that can be used in fuel cells to generate electricity to power vehicles

examples.x: Hydrogen fuel cells convert hydrogen into electricity to power electric vehicles.
She filled up her hydrogen fuel cell vehicle at the hydrogen station.
Hydrogen is produced through electrolysis or steam reforming.
They installed hydrogen refueling stations along major highways.
Hydrogen vehicles emit only water vapor as exhaust.

senses.definition: a type of liquefied petroleum gas used as a fuel in vehicles

examples.x: They converted their vehicle to run on propane for cost savings.
She refilled the propane tank at the local gas station.
Propane is stored as a liquid under pressure in a special tank.
The propane fuel system required regular maintenance.
Propane is a clean-burning alternative fuel for trucks and buses.

senses.definition: a type of alcohol fuel produced from natural gas, coal, or biomass

examples.x: Methanol is used as an alternative fuel in some racing cars.
She researched the environmental benefits of methanol as a fuel.
The methanol fuel station offered competitive prices.
They blended methanol with gasoline for improved engine performance.
Methanol production involves chemical synthesis from renewable sources.

senses.definition: any fuel that can be used instead of fossil fuels

examples.x: Many people are switching to cars that run on alternative fuel to reduce pollution.
Some buses in the city now use alternative fuel like natural gas instead of diesel.
The government is offering incentives for using vehicles powered by alternative fuel.
Scientists are working on making alternative fuel more affordable for everyday drivers.
Electric cars are becoming popular as an alternative fuel option.

senses.definition: a type of fuel produced from renewable or non-renewable resources through chemical processes

examples.x: Many countries are investing in synthetic fuel technology to reduce their dependency on traditional fossil fuels.
Synthetic fuel can be created from various sources, including biomass, coal, or even captured carbon dioxide.
The production of synthetic fuel aims to mitigate environmental impact by offering cleaner alternatives to conventional gasoline and diesel.
Engineers are exploring ways to improve the efficiency and scalability of synthetic fuel production methods.
Some experts believe that synthetic fuel could play a crucial role in future energy strategies, particularly in transportation sectors.

senses.definition: a type of fuel made from living matter, such as plants or waste, that can be used as a renewable energy source

examples.x: Researchers are developing new methods to produce biofuel from algae, aiming for a more sustainable and eco-friendly energy source.
The airline industry is exploring the use of biofuel to reduce carbon emissions from commercial flights.
The government provided incentives for farmers to grow crops specifically for biofuel production, boosting the local economy.
Advances in technology have made it possible to convert used cooking oil into biofuel, which can be used to power diesel engines.

senses.definition: a renewable, alternative fuel made from organic materials such as vegetable oils, animal fats, or recycled cooking grease

examples.x: The truck runs on biodiesel, which is better for the environment than regular diesel.
Some countries offer tax incentives for businesses that use biodiesel.

senses.definition: a liquid found deep under the ground that is used as a fuel
a source of power used for lighting, heating, and operating machines

examples.x: During the storm, we lost electricity for a few hours.
It's important to save electricity by turning off lights when not in use.
Power plants generate electricity that is distributed to homes and businesses.
Solar panels can generate electricity from sunlight.
We use electricity to power the lights in our house.

senses.definition: a place that sells fuel for cars, buses, bikes, etc.

examples.x: They stopped at the gas station to fill up the tank before continuing their road trip.
The gas station on the corner is open 24 hours for convenience.
She bought snacks and drinks from the convenience store at the gas station.
They had their car washed at the gas station while refueling.
He checked the tire pressure at the gas station's air pump.

senses.definition: a place where vehicles can refuel their tanks with gasoline or diesel fuel

examples.x: We stopped at the filling station to refuel the car.
The nearest filling station is about two miles away from here.
The filling station also has a convenience store for snacks.
They waited in line at the filling station for their turn to refuel.
He filled the tank at the filling station before continuing the long drive.

senses.definition: a place where electric vehicles can be recharged

examples.x: The city installed several new charging stations to encourage people to use electric cars.
We need to find a charging station soon because the battery level is getting low.
The company's parking lot now has charging stations for employees who drive electric cars.
She plugged her electric scooter into the charging station before heading into the store.
The company plans to build a network of charging stations across the country to support electric vehicle owners.

senses.definition: to add enough fuel to completely fill the tank of a vehicle

examples.x: I need to fill up the car before we head out on our road trip.
Can you stop at the gas station and fill up the truck on your way home?
It's best to fill up your tank when gas prices are lower.

senses.definition: to fill a vehicle or container with fuel or liquid

examples.x: Yesterday, I tanked up my car before the long journey.
He always tanks up his motorcycle at this gas station because it's cheaper.
By tomorrow, they will have tanked up the boat for the fishing trip.
She is tanking up the lawnmower with gasoline before mowing the lawn.
Please remember to tank up the generator before the power outage hits.

senses.definition: to replenish the fuel supply of a vehicle or machine
a device at gas stations used to dispense gasoline into vehicles

examples.x: He pulled up to the gasoline pump to fill up his tank.
She paid at the gasoline pump using her credit card.
They waited in line at the gasoline pump during rush hour.
The gasoline pump nozzle clicked when the tank was full.
The gasoline pump displayed the price per gallon on the screen.

senses.definition: a device used to recharge electric vehicles

examples.x: Many cities are installing electric vehicle chargers to support the growing number of electric cars on the roads.

senses.definition: designed to use less fuel to do the same work

examples.x: The new car model is highly fuel-efficient, saving money on gas.
They opted for a fuel-efficient furnace to reduce heating costs.
Fuel-efficient appliances are becoming more popular in households.
The airline invested in fuel-efficient aircraft to cut down on fuel expenses.
Choosing a fuel-efficient vehicle can significantly lower your carbon footprint.
